heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: On 09 November 2025, the rain lasted for 144.8 hours, which is the longest time recorded by our database.  47.89mm accumulated and it fell at 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 225 on 28 May 2026.

storm.status: No storm activity at the moment.  The previous storm occurred on 18 July 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest dry periods recorded.  This dry period is ranked 1st since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 03 July 2026 and has lasted 21 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 3 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 27.7°C from 24 April 2026 (when the temperature reached 23.2°C but fell to -4.5°C).

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwave.  This year has seen 3 heatwaves; typically, 1.9 heatwaves happen every year.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 28 June 2026, making a total of only 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (209°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 6.9°C) and outside temperature (down 5.6°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 17%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 21597 lux) and cloud levels (up 1855 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 24806 lux) and cloud levels (up 2321 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 1855.2m lower than yesterday, 2320m lower than the past week, 1736.9m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1536m minimum and 6090m maximum, with an average of 2030m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 4360m and 3770m.
cloud levels.summary: The sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine.

summary.summary: Inside; hot temperatures are forecast. Outside; mild and pleasant. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and little or no wind. Possible light showers, the sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine, and overcast conditions will limit sunlight. Similar weather conditions should remain.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
